# Roadmap: Rune

## Milestones

- ✅ **v1.0 Documentation & Implementation Plan** — Phases 1-3 (shipped 2026-03-02)
- ✅ **v2.0 Repo Restructuring & Scaffold** — Phases 4-7 (shipped 2026-03-03)
- ✅ **v3.0 Scientific Article Documentation** — Phases 8-12 (shipped 2026-03-03)
- ✅ **v4.0 API Wireframes & TDD Foundation** — Phases 13-17 (shipped 2026-03-05)
- 🚧 **v5.0 First Implementation** — Phases 18-22 (in progress)

## Phases

<details>
<summary>✅ v1.0 Documentation & Implementation Plan (Phases 1-3) — SHIPPED 2026-03-02</summary>

- [x] Phase 1: README (1/1 plans) — completed 2026-03-02
- [x] Phase 2: Implementation Plan (2/2 plans) — completed 2026-03-02
- [x] Phase 3: Architecture Docs (1/1 plans) — completed 2026-03-02

</details>

<details>
<summary>✅ v2.0 Repo Restructuring & Scaffold (Phases 4-7) — SHIPPED 2026-03-03</summary>

- [x] Phase 4: Cleanup (3/3 plans) — completed 2026-03-02
- [x] Phase 5: Foundation Libraries (3/3 plans) — completed 2026-03-02
- [x] Phase 5.1: Template Artifact Cleanup (2/2 plans) — completed 2026-03-03
- [x] Phase 6: Service Scaffolds (4/4 plans) — completed 2026-03-03
- [x] Phase 7: Configuration & Quality Gate (3/3 plans) — completed 2026-03-03

</details>

<details>
<summary>✅ v3.0 Scientific Article Documentation (Phases 8-12) — SHIPPED 2026-03-03</summary>

- [x] Phase 8: MkDocs Infrastructure (2/2 plans) — completed 2026-03-03
- [x] Phase 9: References Skeleton + Background (2/2 plans) — completed 2026-03-03
- [x] Phase 10: Methods Section (1/1 plan) — completed 2026-03-03
- [x] Phase 11: Results & Discussion Outlines (2/2 plans) — completed 2026-03-03
- [x] Phase 12: Abstract + Quality Audit (2/2 plans) — completed 2026-03-03

</details>

<details>
<summary>✅ v4.0 API Wireframes & TDD Foundation (Phases 13-17) — SHIPPED 2026-03-05</summary>

- [x] Phase 13: Test Infrastructure (2/2 plans) — completed 2026-03-03
- [x] Phase 14: Core Library Wireframes (3/3 plans) — completed 2026-03-03
- [x] Phase 15: New & Reworked Library Wireframes (2/2 plans) — completed 2026-03-03
- [x] Phase 16: Service Wireframes (4/4 plans) — completed 2026-03-03
- [x] Phase 17: Quality Gate (2/2 plans) — completed 2026-03-04

</details>

### v5.0 First Implementation (In Progress)

**Milestone Goal:** Transform wireframe stubs into a working end-to-end system — adapter registry to provider-agnostic inference to agent loop to QLoRA training to Doc-to-LoRA kill-switch gate — running entirely on local hardware.

- [x] **Phase 18: Adapter Registry** — SQLite-backed CRUD hub that all other components read from and write to (2/2 plans complete)
- [x] **Phase 19: Inference Provider Abstraction** — Abstract InferenceProvider interface with vLLM and Ollama implementations, provider factory, and lora-server configuration (3/3 plans complete)
- [ ] **Phase 20: Agent Loop** — Backend-agnostic generate → execute → reflect → save_trajectory cycle with trajectory persistence (2 plans)
- [ ] **Phase 21: QLoRA Training Pipeline** — Full gradient-descent training path from trajectory to PEFT adapter stored in registry, with training-svc HTTP dispatch
- [ ] **Phase 22: Kill-Switch Gate** — Doc-to-LoRA hypernetwork + evaluation lib measuring the 5% Pass@1 improvement threshold

## Phase Details

### Phase 18: Adapter Registry
**Goal**: Users can persist, retrieve, and query LoRA adapter metadata via a SQLite-backed registry that all other components share
**Depends on**: Nothing (first v5.0 phase)
**Requirements**: AREG-01, AREG-02, AREG-03, AREG-04, AREG-05
**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
  1. User can call AdapterRegistry.store() with full metadata and retrieve the same record by ID with no data loss
  2. User can call AdapterRegistry.query_by_task_type() and receive only adapters matching the specified task type
  3. User can call AdapterRegistry.list_all() and receive all non-archived records (archived records excluded)
  4. Multiple FastAPI requests can read and write concurrently without deadlock (WAL mode confirmed via PRAGMA)
  5. AdapterRegistry constructor accepts an explicit engine parameter so all services share one SQLite file via DATABASE_URL
**Plans**: 2 plans

Plans:
- [x] 18-01-PLAN.md — Implement AdapterRegistry: constructor (Engine + WAL hook + create_all) and 4 CRUD methods — DONE 2026-03-05
- [x] 18-02-PLAN.md — Pivot tests from red to green: update conftest fixtures, replace NotImplementedError assertions, add WAL and concurrency integration tests — DONE 2026-03-05

### Phase 19: Inference Provider Abstraction
**Goal**: Users can generate completions and hot-load/unload LoRA adapters through a provider-agnostic interface, with vLLM and Ollama backends selectable by configuration and a correctly configured lora-server
**Depends on**: Phase 18
**Requirements**: INF-01, INF-02, INF-03, INF-04, INF-05, INF-06, INF-07, INFRA-01, INFRA-02, INFRA-03
**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
  1. InferenceProvider interface exists with generate(), load_adapter(), unload_adapter(), and list_adapters() methods that both backends implement
  2. Provider factory returns a VLLMProvider or OllamaProvider instance based on an environment variable or config file with no code changes required
  3. User can call provider.generate() with an adapter name and receive adapter-conditioned output from the active backend
  4. User can load two adapters simultaneously via VLLMProvider and generate from each by name without collision
  5. Agent can configure different models or providers per step (e.g., VLLMProvider for generation, OllamaProvider for reflection) without changing agent graph structure
  6. lora-server starts without error with VLLM_ALLOW_RUNTIME_LORA_UPDATING=True set, and docker-compose runs all services without port conflict
**Plans**: 3 plans

Plans:
- [x] 19-01-PLAN.md — InferenceProvider ABC + GenerationResult + VLLMProvider + OllamaProvider with full test coverage — DONE 2026-03-05
- [x] 19-02-PLAN.md — lora-server Dockerfile update (vLLM base image + runtime LoRA env) and docker-compose port conflict fix — DONE 2026-03-05
- [x] 19-03-PLAN.md — Provider factory with instance cache + per-step config + __init__.py exports + delete old stubs — DONE 2026-03-05

### Phase 20: Agent Loop
**Goal**: Users can invoke the Rune agent on a coding task and observe a complete generate → execute → reflect → save_trajectory cycle through the InferenceProvider interface, with trajectory data persisted to disk
**Depends on**: Phase 18, Phase 19
**Requirements**: AGENT-01, AGENT-02, AGENT-03, AGENT-04, AGENT-05, AGENT-06, TRAIN-01, TRAIN-02
**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
  1. Agent invocation on a coding task produces generated code from generate_node via InferenceProvider.generate() (backend-agnostic — same call path for vLLM or Ollama)
  2. execute_node runs the generated code in a subprocess sandbox and returns stdout, stderr, exit_code, and tests_passed within the timeout
  3. reflect_node accumulates attempt count and trajectory data without making any LLM call
  4. save_trajectory_node writes a structured JSON trajectory file (session_id, steps, outcome) readable after the session ends
  5. Agent loop closes end-to-end: retries on failure up to max_attempts, then terminates with either success or exhausted outcome; RuneState includes session_id field
**Plans**: 2 plans

Plans:
- [ ] 20-01-PLAN.md — Trajectory library (record, load, format_for_sft) + RuneState session_id + model-training workspace dep
- [ ] 20-02-PLAN.md — All 4 node implementations (generate, execute, reflect, save_trajectory) + green-phase test rewrites

### Phase 21: QLoRA Training Pipeline
**Goal**: Users can trigger end-to-end QLoRA training from a recorded trajectory, producing a PEFT safetensors adapter stored in the registry and accessible for loading via the inference provider
**Depends on**: Phase 18, Phase 20
**Requirements**: TRAIN-03, TRAIN-04, TRAIN-05, TRAIN-06, TRAIN-07, INFRA-04, INFRA-05
**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
  1. User can call build_qlora_config() and receive a PEFT LoraConfig with NF4 quantization and bfloat16 compute dtype
  2. User can call apply_lora_adapter() to attach a LoRA adapter to a base model for inference or further training
  3. POST /train/lora with a session_id runs the full trajectory → SFT format → PEFT train → save safetensors → registry.store() pipeline and returns a job_id
  4. Trained adapter files are in standard PEFT safetensors format (no embed_tokens keys) compatible with vLLM dynamic loading via InferenceProvider.load_adapter()
  5. GPU imports (peft, bitsandbytes, transformers, trl, datasets) are deferred inside function bodies and do not cause ImportError in CPU-only CI
**Plans**: TBD

### Phase 22: Kill-Switch Gate
**Goal**: Users can measure whether Doc-to-LoRA-generated adapters improve Pass@1 on a HumanEval subset by at least 5% relative to the base model, validating the core hypothesis
**Depends on**: Phase 20, Phase 21
**Requirements**: DTOL-01, DTOL-02, DTOL-03, DTOL-04, EVAL-01, EVAL-02, EVAL-03
**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
  1. DocToLoraHypernetwork exists in model-training lib, accepts a coding trajectory, and returns rank-8 LoRA adapter weights in a single forward pass under 1 second
  2. Generated adapter weights are in standard PEFT safetensors format and can be loaded into vLLM via InferenceProvider.load_adapter() without error
  3. User can call run_humaneval_subset() and receive pass/fail results for each task in the 20-task subset
  4. User can call calculate_pass_at_k() and receive a Pass@1 score for both baseline and adapter-enhanced configurations
  5. Kill-switch gate comparison prints a clear verdict: PASS (>=5% improvement) or FAIL (hypothesis rejected), with both baseline and adapter Pass@1 scores shown
**Plans**: TBD

## Progress

|-------|-----------|----------------|--------|-----------|
| 1. README | v1.0 | 1/1 | Complete | 2026-03-02 |
| 2. Implementation Plan | v1.0 | 2/2 | Complete | 2026-03-02 |

| 16. Service Wireframes | v4.0 | 4/4 | Complete | 2026-03-03 |
| 17. Quality Gate | v4.0 | 2/2 | Complete | 2026-03-04 |
| 18. Adapter Registry | v5.0 | Complete    | 2026-03-05 | 2026-03-05 |
| 19. Inference Provider Abstraction | v5.0 | Complete    | 2026-03-05 | 2026-03-05 |
| 20. Agent Loop | v5.0 | 0/2 | Planned | - |
| 21. QLoRA Training Pipeline | v5.0 | 0/TBD | Not started | - |
| 22. Kill-Switch Gate | v5.0 | 0/TBD | Not started | - |

## PLANNING COMPLETE

**Phase:** 20-agent-loop
**Plans:** 2 plan(s) in 2 wave(s)

### Wave Structure

| Wave | Plans | Autonomous |
|------|-------|------------|
| 1 | 20-01 | yes |
| 2 | 20-02 | yes |

### Plans Created

| Plan | Objective | Tasks | Files | Requirements |
|------|-----------|-------|-------|--------------|
| 20-01 | Trajectory library (record, load, format_for_sft) + RuneState session_id + model-training workspace dep | 2 | `trajectory.py`, `__init__.py`, `test_trajectory.py`, `state.py`, `pyproject.toml` | AGENT-05, TRAIN-01, TRAIN-02 |
| 20-02 | All 4 node implementations (generate, execute, reflect, save_trajectory) + green-phase test rewrites | 2 | `nodes.py`, `test_nodes.py` | AGENT-01, AGENT-02, AGENT-03, AGENT-04, AGENT-06 |

### Key Design Decisions

1. **Plan 01 (Wave 1)** handles the trajectory persistence library and configuration prep that Plan 02 depends on. It implements `record_trajectory()`, `load_trajectory()`, and `format_for_sft()` in model-training lib, adds `session_id` to `RuneState`, and wires `model-training` as a workspace dependency of `rune-agent`.

2. **Plan 02 (Wave 2)** implements all 4 node functions (`generate_node`, `execute_node`, `reflect_node`, `save_trajectory_node`) and rewrites the red-phase `test_nodes.py` to green-phase behavior tests. It depends on Plan 01 because `save_trajectory_node` imports `record_trajectory()` and reads `state["session_id"]`.

3. **Signature extension for `record_trajectory()`** — The research identified that the current stub signature `(session_id, steps, outcome)` lacks `task_description`, `task_type`, and `adapter_ids` that CONTEXT.md says must be in the JSON. Plan 01 extends the signature with optional kwargs.

4. **No file overlap** between plans (Plan 01 touches model-training + state.py + pyproject.toml; Plan 02 touches only nodes.py + test_nodes.py).

### Requirement Coverage

All 8 requirement IDs covered:
- AGENT-01 through AGENT-06 (Plan 02: AGENT-01/02/03/04/06, Plan 01: AGENT-05)
- TRAIN-01, TRAIN-02 (Plan 01)

### Files Created

- `/Users/noahdolevelixir/Code/rune/.planning/phases/20-agent-loop/20-01-PLAN.md`
- `/Users/noahdolevelixir/Code/rune/.planning/phases/20-agent-loop/20-02-PLAN.md`
- `/Users/noahdolevelixir/Code/rune/.planning/ROADMAP.md` (updated)

## PLANNING COMPLETE

**Phase:** 20-agent-loop
**Plans:** 2 plan(s) in 2 wave(s)

### Wave Structure

| Wave | Plans | Autonomous |
|------|-------|------------|
| 1 | 20-01 | yes |
| 2 | 20-02 | yes |

### Plans Created

| Plan | Objective | Tasks | Files | Requirements |
|------|-----------|-------|-------|--------------|
| 20-01 | Trajectory library (record, load, format_for_sft) + RuneState session_id + model-training workspace dep | 2 | `trajectory.py`, `__init__.py`, `test_trajectory.py`, `state.py`, `pyproject.toml` | AGENT-05, TRAIN-01, TRAIN-02 |
| 20-02 | All 4 node implementations (generate, execute, reflect, save_trajectory) + green-phase test rewrites | 2 | `nodes.py`, `test_nodes.py` | AGENT-01, AGENT-02, AGENT-03, AGENT-04, AGENT-06 |

### Key Design Decisions

1. **Plan 01 (Wave 1)** handles the trajectory persistence library and configuration prep that Plan 02 depends on. It implements `record_trajectory()`, `load_trajectory()`, and `format_for_sft()` in model-training lib, adds `session_id` to `RuneState`, and wires `model-training` as a workspace dependency of `rune-agent`.

2. **Plan 02 (Wave 2)** implements all 4 node functions (`generate_node`, `execute_node`, `reflect_node`, `save_trajectory_node`) and rewrites the red-phase `test_nodes.py` to green-phase behavior tests. It depends on Plan 01 because `save_trajectory_node` imports `record_trajectory()` and reads `state["session_id"]`.

3. **Signature extension for `record_trajectory()`** — The research identified that the current stub signature `(session_id, steps, outcome)` lacks `task_description`, `task_type`, and `adapter_ids` that CONTEXT.md says must be in the JSON. Plan 01 extends the signature with optional kwargs.

4. **No file overlap** between plans (Plan 01 touches model-training + state.py + pyproject.toml; Plan 02 touches only nodes.py + test_nodes.py).

### Requirement Coverage

All 8 requirement IDs covered:
- AGENT-01 through AGENT-06 (Plan 02: AGENT-01/02/03/04/06, Plan 01: AGENT-05)
- TRAIN-01, TRAIN-02 (Plan 01)

### Files Created

- `/Users/noahdolevelixir/Code/rune/.planning/phases/20-agent-loop/20-01-PLAN.md`
- `/Users/noahdolevelixir/Code/rune/.planning/phases/20-agent-loop/20-02-PLAN.md`
- `/Users/noahdolevelixir/Code/rune/.planning/ROADMAP.md` (updated)

    68→**Goal**: Users can persist, retrieve, and query LoRA adapter metadata via a SQLite-backed registry that all other components share

    74→  3. User can call AdapterRegistry.list_all() and receive all non-archived records (archived records excluded)
    75→  4. Multiple FastAPI requests can read and write concurrently without deadlock (WAL mode confirmed via PRAGMA)
    76→  5. AdapterRegistry constructor accepts an explicit engine parameter so all services share one SQLite file via DATABASE_URL
    77→**Plans**: 2 plans

    83→### Phase 19: Inference Provider Abstraction
    84→**Goal**: Users can generate completions and hot-load/unload LoRA adapters through a provider-agnostic interface, with vLLM and Ollama backends selectable by configuration and a correctly configured lora-server
    85→**Depends on**: Phase 18

    89→  2. Provider factory returns a VLLMProvider or OllamaProvider instance based on an environment variable or config file with no code changes required
    90→  3. User can call provider.generate() with an adapter name and receive adapter-conditioned output from the active backend
    91→  4. User can load two adapters simultaneously via VLLMProvider and generate from each by name without collision
    92→  5. Agent can configure different models or providers per step (e.g., VLLMProvider for generation, OllamaProvider for reflection) without changing agent graph structure
    93→  6. lora-server starts without error with VLLM_ALLOW_RUNTIME_LORA_UPDATING=True set, and docker-compose runs all services without port conflict
    94→**Plans**: 3 plans

   102→**Goal**: Users can invoke the Rune agent on a coding task and observe a complete generate → execute → reflect → save_trajectory cycle through the InferenceProvider interface, with trajectory data persisted to disk

   105→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
   106→  1. Agent invocation on a coding task produces generated code from generate_node via InferenceProvider.generate() (backend-agnostic — same call path for vLLM or Ollama)
   107→  2. execute_node runs the generated code in a subprocess sandbox and returns stdout, stderr, exit_code, and tests_passed within the timeout
   108→  3. reflect_node accumulates attempt count and trajectory data without making any LLM call
   109→  4. save_trajectory_node writes a structured JSON trajectory file (session_id, steps, outcome) readable after the session ends
   110→  5. Agent loop closes end-to-end: retries on failure up to max_attempts, then terminates with either success or exhausted outcome; RuneState includes session_id field
   111→**Plans**: 2 plans

   113→Plans:
   114→- [ ] 20-01-PLAN.md — Trajectory library (record, load, format_for_sft) + RuneState session_id + model-training workspace dep
   115→- [ ] 20-02-PLAN.md — All 4 node implementations (generate, execute, reflect, save_trajectory) + green-phase test rewrites
   116→
   117→### Phase 21: QLoRA Training Pipeline
   118→**Goal**: Users can trigger end-to-end QLoRA training from a recorded trajectory, producing a PEFT safetensors adapter stored in the registry and accessible for loading via the inference provider
   119→**Depends on**: Phase 18, Phase 20
   120→**Requirements**: TRAIN-03, TRAIN-04, TRAIN-05, TRAIN-06, TRAIN-07, INFRA-04, INFRA-05
   121→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
   122→  1. User can call build_qlora_config() and receive a PEFT LoraConfig with NF4 quantization and bfloat16 compute dtype
   123→  2. User can call apply_lora_adapter() to attach a LoRA adapter to a base model for inference or further training
   124→  3. POST /train/lora with a session_id runs the full trajectory → SFT format → PEFT train → save safetensors → registry.store() pipeline and returns a job_id
   125→  4. Trained adapter files are in standard PEFT safetensors format (no embed_tokens keys) compatible with vLLM dynamic loading via InferenceProvider.load_adapter()
   126→  5. GPU imports (peft, bitsandbytes, transformers, trl, datasets) are deferred inside function bodies and do not cause ImportError in CPU-only CI
   127→**Plans**: TBD
   128→
   129→### Phase 22: Kill-Switch Gate
   130→**Goal**: Users can measure whether Doc-to-LoRA-generated adapters improve Pass@1 on a HumanEval subset by at least 5% relative to the base model, validating the core hypothesis
   131→**Depends on**: Phase 20, Phase 21
   132→**Requirements**: DTOL-01, DTOL-02, DTOL-03, DTOL-04, EVAL-01, EVAL-02, EVAL-03
   133→**Success Criteria** (what must be TRUE):
   134→  1. DocToLoraHypernetwork exists in model-training lib, accepts a coding trajectory, and returns rank-8 LoRA adapter weights in a single forward pass under 1 second
   135→  2. Generated adapter weights are in standard PEFT safetensors format and can be loaded into vLLM via InferenceProvider.load_adapter() without error
   136→  3. User can call run_humaneval_subset() and receive pass/fail results for each task in the 20-task subset
   137→  4. User can call calculate_pass_at_k() and receive a Pass@1 score for both baseline and adapter-enhanced configurations
   138→  5. Kill-switch gate comparison prints a clear verdict: PASS (>=5% improvement) or FAIL (hypothesis rejected), with both baseline and adapter Pass@1 scores shown
   139→**Plans**: TBD

1→# Requirements: Rune
     2→
     3→**Defined:** 2026-03-05
     4→**Core Value:** A local coding agent that learns from its own coding trajectories, building persistent parametric memory that scales independently of context window size.
     5→
     6→## v5.0 Requirements
     7→
     8→Requirements for first working implementation. Each maps to roadmap phases.
     9→
    10→### Adapter Registry
    11→
    12→- [x] **AREG-01**: User can store a new adapter record with metadata (task_type, base_model_id, rank, file_path, file_hash, source) via AdapterRegistry.store()
    13→- [x] **AREG-02**: User can retrieve an adapter record by ID via AdapterRegistry.retrieve_by_id()
    14→- [x] **AREG-03**: User can query adapters by task_type via AdapterRegistry.query_by_task_type()
    15→- [x] **AREG-04**: User can list all non-archived adapters via AdapterRegistry.list_all()
    16→- [x] **AREG-05**: AdapterRegistry uses SQLite with WAL mode and engine-parameterized constructor for shared database access
    17→
    18→### Inference
    19→
    20→- [x] **INF-01**: Abstract InferenceProvider interface with generate(), load_adapter(), unload_adapter(), list_adapters() methods
    21→- [x] **INF-02**: VLLMProvider implementation with full LoRA hot-loading support (POST /v1/load_lora_adapter, /v1/unload_lora_adapter)
    22→- [x] **INF-03**: OllamaProvider implementation for inference via Ollama API (LoRA support where Ollama supports it)
    23→- [x] **INF-04**: Provider factory/registry for selecting backend by configuration (env var or config file)
    24→- [x] **INF-05**: User can generate with a specific loaded adapter by passing adapter name as model parameter
    25→- [x] **INF-06**: User can load multiple adapters simultaneously for composition (provider-dependent, graceful degradation)
    26→- [x] **INF-07**: Per-step model/provider configuration — agent can use different models or providers for different steps (e.g., one model for generation, another for reflection)
    27→
    28→### Agent Loop
    29→
    30→- [ ] **AGENT-01**: generate_node calls InferenceProvider.generate() with task description and optional adapter, returning generated code (backend-agnostic)
    31→- [ ] **AGENT-02**: execute_node runs generated code in a sandboxed subprocess with timeout, returning stdout/stderr/exit_code/tests_passed
    32→- [ ] **AGENT-03**: reflect_node accumulates trajectory data (attempt count, code, results) without LLM call
    33→- [ ] **AGENT-04**: save_trajectory_node persists trajectory via record_trajectory() and sets outcome
    34→- [ ] **AGENT-05**: RuneState includes session_id field for trajectory persistence
    35→- [ ] **AGENT-06**: Agent loop closes end-to-end: generate → execute → reflect → retry/save with should_retry routing
    36→
    37→### Training Pipeline
    38→
    39→- [ ] **TRAIN-01**: User can record a coding trajectory as structured JSON via record_trajectory(session_id, steps, outcome)
    40→- [ ] **TRAIN-02**: User can convert trajectory data to SFT chat format via format_for_sft()
    41→- [ ] **TRAIN-03**: User can create a QLoRA config with NF4 quantization and bfloat16 compute dtype via build_qlora_config()
    42→- [ ] **TRAIN-04**: User can apply a LoRA adapter to a base model via apply_lora_adapter()
    43→- [ ] **TRAIN-05**: QLoRA training pipeline runs end-to-end: trajectory → SFT format → PEFT train → save safetensors → store in registry
    44→- [ ] **TRAIN-06**: training-svc exposes POST /train/lora endpoint with async background job tracking
    45→- [ ] **TRAIN-07**: training-svc pyproject.toml declares model-training as workspace dependency
    46→
    47→### Doc-to-LoRA Hypernetwork
    48→
    49→- [ ] **DTOL-01**: DocToLoraHypernetwork module exists in model-training lib with Perceiver-based architecture
    50→- [ ] **DTOL-02**: Hypernetwork generates rank-8 LoRA adapter weights from coding trajectory in a single forward pass (<1s)
    51→- [ ] **DTOL-03**: Generated adapters are compatible with vLLM dynamic LoRA loading (standard PEFT safetensors format)
    52→- [ ] **DTOL-04**: training-svc exposes POST /train/hypernetwork endpoint for Doc-to-LoRA training
    53→
    54→### Evaluation & Kill-Switch
    55→
    56→- [ ] **EVAL-01**: User can run a HumanEval subset benchmark via run_humaneval_subset()
    57→- [ ] **EVAL-02**: User can calculate Pass@k metrics via calculate_pass_at_k()
    58→- [ ] **EVAL-03**: Kill-switch gate compares baseline vs adapter-enhanced Pass@1 (5% improvement threshold)
    59→
    60→### Infrastructure
    61→
    62→- [x] **INFRA-01**: lora-server Dockerfile uses vllm/vllm-openai:v0.16.0 base image (not python:3.12-slim)
    63→- [x] **INFRA-02**: lora-server sets VLLM_ALLOW_RUNTIME_LORA_UPDATING=True environment variable
    64→- [x] **INFRA-03**: docker-compose resolves port conflict (api-service and lora-server on different host ports)
    65→- [ ] **INFRA-04**: model-training pyproject.toml adds GPU dependencies (peft, bitsandbytes, transformers, trl, datasets) with TYPE_CHECKING guards
    66→- [ ] **INFRA-05**: All GPU imports deferred inside function bodies (not top-level) for CPU-only CI compatibility
    67→
    68→## Future Requirements
    69→
    70→Deferred to v6+. Tracked but not in current roadmap.
    71→
    72→### Evolution
    73→
    74→- **EVOL-01**: Evolution operator scores adapter fitness based on HumanEval pass rate
    75→- **EVOL-02**: Evolution operator performs adapter crossover via weight interpolation
    76→- **EVOL-03**: Evolution operator archives low-fitness adapters
    77→
    78→### Advanced Features
    79→
    80→- **ADV-01**: Embedding-based automatic adapter selection via vector similarity search
    81→- **ADV-02**: HITL UI with session monitoring, adapter library browser, trajectory viewer
    82→- **ADV-03**: Docker container isolation for code execution (replace subprocess sandbox)
    83→
    84→## Out of Scope
    85→
    86→Explicitly excluded. Documented to prevent scope creep.
    87→
    88→| Feature | Reason |
    89→|---------|--------|
    90→| Hardware validation phase | User deferred — will validate hardware separately |
    91→| Tensor parallelism (TP=2) | vLLM bug #21471 — corrupted output on PCIe GPUs without NVLink |
    92→| Adapter merging into base weights | Loses hot-swap composability; vLLM Punica kernels handle LoRA with near-zero overhead |
    93→| Cloud API inference | Hard constraint: local-first, no cloud dependencies for inference. Provider abstraction supports local backends (vLLM, Ollama) only |
    94→| Concurrent training + inference | CUDA OOM — must schedule sequentially |
    95→| Streaming responses in agent loop | Agent needs complete code, not token stream; add to API layer separately if needed |
    96→| Multi-tenant isolation | Single-user local system; session_id provides sufficient organization |
    97→| evolution-svc implementation | Depends on populated adapter library from multiple sessions; v6+ |
    98→

1→# Phase 20: Agent Loop - Context
     2→
     3→**Gathered:** 2026-03-05
     4→**Status:** Ready for planning
     5→
     6→<domain>
     7→## Phase Boundary
     8→
     9→Backend-agnostic generate → execute → reflect → save_trajectory cycle. Agent invokes InferenceProvider.generate() for code generation, runs code in a sandboxed subprocess, accumulates trajectory data, and persists structured JSON trajectories to disk. Also implements trajectory recording and SFT format conversion in model-training lib. Graph topology (should_retry, create_graph) is already implemented — this phase fills in the node implementations and trajectory persistence.
    10→
    11→</domain>
    12→
    13→<decisions>
    14→## Implementation Decisions
    15→
    16→### Subprocess sandbox
    17→- Simple `subprocess.run()` with configurable timeout — no file/network restrictions at this stage
    18→- Docker container isolation deferred to ADV-03
    19→- Default timeout: 30 seconds, configurable via RuneState or env var
    20→- Exit code determines tests_passed: `tests_passed = (exit_code == 0)`
    21→- Concatenate generated_code + test_suite into a single .py file, run it — mirrors HumanEval evaluation pattern
    22→- Run in a fresh temp directory (tmpdir) to prevent accidental writes to the project tree
    23→
    24→### Prompt construction
    25→- First attempt prompt includes both task_description AND test_suite — model sees the tests it needs to pass
    26→- System prompt sets the role (e.g., "You are a Python code generator. Output only code, no explanation.") + user prompt contains task + tests
    27→- On retry attempts, append prior generated_code + stdout + stderr + exit_code to the prompt — classic self-repair pattern
    28→- Extract code from ```python ... ``` blocks in the response; fallback: treat entire response as code if no blocks found
    29→
    30→### Trajectory storage
    31→- Storage directory: `~/.rune/trajectories/` — consistent with `~/.rune/registry.db` from Phase 18
    32→- Configurable via `RUNE_TRAJECTORY_DIR` env var
    33→- File naming: `{session_id}.json` — one file per session, session_id is UUID so no collisions
    34→- Session ID created by caller (uuid4 at agent start), passed through RuneState — record_trajectory() receives it, doesn't generate it
    35→- Session-level metadata stored: session_id, task_description, task_type, adapter_ids, outcome, timestamp, steps[]
    36→- Each step contains: generated_code, stdout, stderr, exit_code, tests_passed — mirrors RuneState fields
    37→
    38→### SFT format mapping
    39→- Task as user message, successful code as assistant message — standard SFT chat pattern
    40→- System prompt included in output (role: "system") — trains model with same prompt structure used at inference
    41→- Only successful trajectories formatted (outcome="success") — failed trajectories are noise for SFT
    42→- Final successful attempt only — single user→assistant turn, not full retry chain
    43→- format_for_sft() returns `list[dict[str, str]]` with role/content keys per existing signature
    44→
    45→### Claude's Discretion
    46→- Exact system prompt wording for generate_node
    47→- Code block extraction regex details
    48→- Temp directory cleanup strategy
    49→- Error message formatting in trajectory steps
    50→- Whether to add model-training as workspace dep for rune-agent or import via shared path
    51→
    52→</decisions>
    53→
    54→<specifics>
    55→## Specific Ideas
    56→
    57→- Concatenate + run pattern mirrors HumanEval evaluation — generated function definitions followed by assert-based test suite in one script
    58→- Self-repair on retry: prompt includes "Your previous attempt produced the following errors:" + stderr/stdout — standard approach from self-repair literature
    59→- Trajectory JSON should be human-readable (json.dumps with indent=2) for debugging
    60→
    61→</specifics>
    62→

    68→- `get_provider()` / `get_provider_for_step()`: factory with (type, url) cache — agent obtains providers through these
    69→- `should_retry()` + `create_graph()`: already implemented in graph.py — graph topology is locked

    76→- Env var for service discovery: `INFERENCE_PROVIDER`, `VLLM_BASE_URL`, `DATABASE_URL` — follow with `RUNE_TRAJECTORY_DIR`

    82→- `rune-agent/nodes.py` → `model_training.record_trajectory()` for trajectory persistence

     7→## Summary
     8→
     9→Phase 20 fills in four node stubs (`generate_node`, `execute_node`, `reflect_node`, `save_trajectory_node`) in `rune-agent`, implements three trajectory functions (`record_trajectory`, `load_trajectory`, `format_for_sft`) in `model-training`, adds `session_id` to `RuneState`, and wires `model-training` as a workspace dependency of `rune-agent`. The LangGraph graph topology (`should_retry`, `create_graph`) is already complete and tested. All other infrastructure — `InferenceProvider`, `get_provider()`, `get_provider_for_step()` — is fully implemented in Phase 19. This phase is pure implementation against locked designs with no architectural discovery needed.
    10→
    11→The key integration point is that `save_trajectory_node` calls `record_trajectory()` from `model-training`, so `rune-agent` must declare `model-training` as a workspace dep in its `pyproject.toml`. The node functions are `async`; the trajectory functions are sync file I/O. The existing red-phase tests (4 in `test_nodes.py`, 3 in `test_trajectory.py`) become green — tests change from asserting `NotImplementedError` to asserting correct behavior.
    12→
    13→**Primary recommendation:** Implement nodes and trajectory functions in sequence (trajectory lib first, then nodes), with `session_id` field addition and `pyproject.toml` update as the first two prep steps.

    76→## Standard Stack
    77→
    78→### Core
    79→| Library | Version | Purpose | Why Standard |
    80→|---------|---------|---------|--------------|

    83→| `model-training` (workspace) | local | trajectory functions | Needs to be added as workspace dep to rune-agent |
    84→| `subprocess` | stdlib | Code execution sandbox | stdlib; no extra dep needed |
    85→| `tempfile` | stdlib | Isolated temp directory for execution | stdlib; `mkdtemp()` + cleanup |
    86→| `re` | stdlib | Code block extraction from LLM response | Extract ```python ... ``` blocks |
    87→| `json` | stdlib | Trajectory file serialization | `json.dumps(indent=2)` for human-readable files |
    88→| `pathlib` | stdlib | `~/.rune/trajectories/` directory management | Consistent with Phase 18 pattern |

    97→
    98→### Alternatives Considered
    99→| Instead of | Could Use | Tradeoff |
   100→|------------|-----------|----------|
   101→| `subprocess.run()` | `asyncio.create_subprocess_exec` | Async subprocess would let the event loop handle timeout; but sync subprocess in async node is simpler, and the 30s timeout means the loop blocks at most 30s per attempt — acceptable for a local coding agent |
   102→| Flat JSON files | SQLite table | JSON files are trivially readable/debuggable; CONTEXT.md locked this choice |
   103→| `tempfile.mkdtemp()` + manual cleanup | `tempfile.TemporaryDirectory` context manager | Context manager auto-cleans; Claude's discretion on cleanup strategy — context manager is cleaner |

   116→
   117→## Architecture Patterns
   118→
   119→### Recommended Project Structure (no changes to structure, only file implementations)
   120→```
   121→services/rune-agent/src/rune_agent/
   122→├── __init__.py          # existing — no change
   123→├── graph.py             # existing complete — no change
   124→├── nodes.py             # IMPLEMENT: 4 node functions
   125→└── state.py             # ADD: session_id field
   126→
   127→libs/model-training/src/model_training/
   128→├── __init__.py          # UPDATE: export trajectory functions
   129→├── config.py            # existing — no change
   130→├── peft_utils.py        # existing — no change
   131→└── trajectory.py        # IMPLEMENT: record_trajectory, load_trajectory, format_for_sft
   132→```
   133→
   134→### Pattern 1: generate_node — Prompt Construction and Code Extraction
   135→
   136→**What:** Build a system+user prompt, call `get_provider().generate()`, extract code from response.
   137→**When to use:** First attempt uses task + tests; retry attempts append prior failure context.
   138→
   139→```python
   140→# Source: CONTEXT.md locked decisions + InferenceProvider interface (provider.py)
   141→import re
   142→from inference.factory import get_provider
   143→from inference.provider import GenerationResult
   144→from .state import RuneState
   145→from typing import Any
   146→
   147→SYSTEM_PROMPT = (
   148→    "You are a Python code generator. "
   149→    "Output only code, no explanation."
   150→)
   151→
   152→def _build_prompt(state: RuneState) -> str:
   153→    if state["attempt_count"] == 0:
   154→        return (
   155→            f"Task: {state['task_description']}\n\n"
   156→            f"Tests:\n{state['test_suite']}\n\n"
   157→            "Write a Python solution that passes all tests."
   158→        )
   159→    # Retry: append prior failure context
   160→    return (
   161→        f"Task: {state['task_description']}\n\n"
   162→        f"Tests:\n{state['test_suite']}\n\n"
   163→        "Your previous attempt produced the following errors:\n"
   164→        f"Code:\n{state['generated_code']}\n"
   165→        f"stdout: {state['stdout']}\n"
   166→        f"stderr: {state['stderr']}\n"
   167→        f"exit_code: {state['exit_code']}\n\n"
   168→        "Write a corrected Python solution that passes all tests."
   169→    )
   170→
   171→def _extract_code(text: str) -> str:
   172→    """Extract code from ```python ... ``` block; fallback to full text."""
   173→    match = re.search(r"```python\s*(.*?)```", text, re.DOTALL)
   174→    if match:
   175→        return match.group(1).strip()
   176→    return text.strip()
   177→
   178→async def generate_node(state: RuneState) -> dict[str, Any]:
   179→    provider = get_provider()
   180→    prompt = _build_prompt(state)
   181→    result: GenerationResult = await provider.generate(
   182→        prompt=prompt,
   183→        model="Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-7B",  # or from config
   184→        adapter_id=state["adapter_ids"][0] if state["adapter_ids"] else None,
   185→    )
   186→    return {"generated_code": _extract_code(result.text)}
   187→```
   188→
   189→### Pattern 2: execute_node — Subprocess Sandbox
   190→
   191→**What:** Concatenate generated_code + test_suite into a temp .py file, run it with subprocess.run(), capture results.
   192→**When to use:** Always — called after generate_node in the graph.
   193→
   194→```python
   195→# Source: CONTEXT.md locked decisions; stdlib subprocess + tempfile
   196→import subprocess
   197→import tempfile
   198→import os
   199→from typing import Any
   200→from .state import RuneState
   201→
   202→DEFAULT_TIMEOUT = 30
   203→
   204→async def execute_node(state: RuneState) -> dict[str, Any]:
   205→    timeout = int(os.environ.get("RUNE_EXEC_TIMEOUT", str(DEFAULT_TIMEOUT)))
   206→    script = state["generated_code"] + "\n\n" + state["test_suite"]
   207→
   208→    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmpdir:
   209→        script_path = os.path.join(tmpdir, "solution.py")
   210→        with open(script_path, "w") as f:
   211→            f.write(script)
   212→
   213→        try:
   214→            proc = subprocess.run(
   215→                ["python", script_path],
   216→                capture_output=True,
   217→                text=True,
   218→                timeout=timeout,
   219→                cwd=tmpdir,
   220→            )
   221→            return {
   222→                "stdout": proc.stdout,
   223→                "stderr": proc.stderr,
   224→                "exit_code": proc.returncode,
   225→                "tests_passed": proc.returncode == 0,
   226→            }
   227→        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
   228→            return {
   229→                "stdout": "",
   230→                "stderr": f"Execution timed out after {timeout}s",
   231→                "exit_code": 1,
   232→                "tests_passed": False,
   233→            }
   234→```
   235→
   236→### Pattern 3: reflect_node — State Accumulation (no LLM call)
   237→
   238→**What:** Increment attempt_count, append current attempt data to trajectory list.
   239→**When to use:** Always — called after execute_node.
   240→
   241→```python
   242→# Source: CONTEXT.md locked decisions; pure dict manipulation
   243→async def reflect_node(state: RuneState) -> dict[str, Any]:
   244→    step = {
   245→        "generated_code": state["generated_code"],
   246→        "stdout": state["stdout"],
   247→        "stderr": state["stderr"],
   248→        "exit_code": state["exit_code"],
   249→        "tests_passed": state["tests_passed"],
   250→    }
   251→    return {
   252→        "attempt_count": state["attempt_count"] + 1,
   253→        "trajectory": state["trajectory"] + [step],
   254→    }
   255→```
   256→
   257→### Pattern 4: save_trajectory_node — Persistence and Outcome
   258→
   259→**What:** Determine outcome (success/exhausted), call record_trajectory(), return outcome.
   260→**When to use:** Terminal node — called by should_retry when loop ends.
   261→
   262→```python
   263→# Source: CONTEXT.md locked decisions
   264→from model_training.trajectory import record_trajectory
   265→
   266→async def save_trajectory_node(state: RuneState) -> dict[str, Any]:
   267→    outcome = "success" if state["tests_passed"] else "exhausted"
   268→    record_trajectory(
   269→        session_id=state["session_id"],
   270→        steps=state["trajectory"],
   271→        outcome=outcome,
   272→    )
   273→    return {"outcome": outcome}
   274→```
   275→
   276→### Pattern 5: record_trajectory — JSON file persistence
   277→
   278→**What:** Write structured trajectory JSON to `~/.rune/trajectories/{session_id}.json`.
   279→**When to use:** Called by save_trajectory_node.
   280→
   281→```python
   282→# Source: CONTEXT.md locked decisions; stdlib json + pathlib
   283→import json
   284→import os
   285→from pathlib import Path
   286→from datetime import datetime, timezone
   287→from typing import Any, Optional
   288→
   289→_DEFAULT_TRAJ_DIR = Path.home() / ".rune" / "trajectories"
   290→
   291→def record_trajectory(
   292→    session_id: str,
   293→    steps: list[dict[str, Any]],
   294→    outcome: Optional[str] = None,
   295→) -> dict[str, Any]:
   296→    traj_dir = Path(
   297→        os.environ.get("RUNE_TRAJECTORY_DIR", str(_DEFAULT_TRAJ_DIR))
   298→    )
   299→    traj_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   300→
   301→    trajectory = {
   302→        "session_id": session_id,
   303→        "outcome": outcome,
   304→        "timestamp": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
   305→        "steps": steps,
   306→    }
   307→    file_path = traj_dir / f"{session_id}.json"
   308→    file_path.write_text(json.dumps(trajectory, indent=2))
   309→
   310→    return {"session_id": session_id, "file_path": str(file_path)}
   311→```
   312→
   313→### Pattern 6: format_for_sft — SFT Chat Format Conversion
   314→
   315→**What:** Convert successful trajectory to system+user+assistant message list.
   316→**When to use:** Only for trajectories with outcome=="success"; returns [] or raises for others.
   317→
   318→```python
   319→# Source: CONTEXT.md locked decisions
   320→def format_for_sft(trajectory: dict[str, Any]) -> list[dict[str, str]]:
   321→    if trajectory.get("outcome") != "success":
   322→        return []
   323→
   324→    # Find the final successful step
   325→    steps = trajectory.get("steps", [])
   326→    successful_step = next(
   327→        (s for s in reversed(steps) if s.get("tests_passed")), None
   328→    )
   329→    if successful_step is None:
   330→        return []
   331→
   332→    return [
   333→        {"role": "system", "content": SYSTEM_PROMPT},
   334→        {"role": "user", "content": trajectory.get("task_description", "")},
   335→        {"role": "assistant", "content": successful_step["generated_code"]},
   336→    ]
   337→```
   338→
   339→### Anti-Patterns to Avoid
   340→- **Running subprocess outside tempdir:** The generated code could write files anywhere; always use `cwd=tmpdir` with a fresh temp directory.
   341→- **Module-level env var reads in nodes.py:** Following the Phase 19 pattern from `factory.py`, read env vars inside function bodies so `monkeypatch.setenv()` works in tests.
   342→- **Calling `get_provider()` at module import time:** Import-time provider construction fails when the server is not running; defer to function call time.
   343→- **Mutating `state["trajectory"]` in place:** LangGraph expects nodes to return state update dicts; always return `trajectory: state["trajectory"] + [step]` (new list), never `.append()`.
   344→- **Returning outcome from reflect_node:** The `outcome` field must only be set by `save_trajectory_node`; reflect_node sets `attempt_count` and `trajectory` only.
   345→
   346→## Don't Hand-Roll
   347→
   348→| Problem | Don't Build | Use Instead | Why |
   349→|---------|-------------|-------------|-----|
   350→| Graph execution/routing | Custom loop with while/recursion | `langgraph.graph.StateGraph` (already used) | Retry routing already implemented in `should_retry()` + `create_graph()` |
   351→| Provider selection | Custom if/else on env vars in nodes | `inference.factory.get_provider()` | Phase 19 factory handles caching, env var resolution, provider instantiation |
   352→| Async HTTP to LLM | Custom httpx calls in generate_node | `InferenceProvider.generate()` | Already abstracts vLLM and Ollama; VLLMProvider handles adapter_id as model param |
   353→| SFT format schema | Custom chat format | The locked `list[dict[str, str]]` with role/content | Matches what TRL/HuggingFace trainers expect directly |
   354→
   355→**Key insight:** The heavy lifting (graph topology, provider abstraction, DB registry) is done. Phase 20 is pure function implementations with no new architectural decisions.
   356→
   357→## Common Pitfalls
   358→
   359→### Pitfall 1: Async node calling sync subprocess incorrectly
   360→**What goes wrong:** Calling `subprocess.run()` inside an async function blocks the event loop for the full timeout duration.
   361→**Why it happens:** LangGraph nodes are async, but `subprocess.run()` is synchronous. For a 30s timeout, this is acceptable (local coding agent, single user, not a server). For future high-throughput use, `asyncio.create_subprocess_exec` would be needed.
   362→**How to avoid:** For Phase 20 with a 30s default, `subprocess.run()` in an async function is acceptable. Document that the loop blocks during execution. Do NOT use `await asyncio.sleep()` or other workarounds — just call `subprocess.run()` directly.
   363→**Warning signs:** If tests using `pytest-asyncio` hang, check if the subprocess is deadlocking (pipes full). Use `capture_output=True`, not `PIPE`, to avoid this; actually `capture_output=True` IS equivalent to `stdout=PIPE, stderr=PIPE` — ensure the process completes.
   364→
   365→### Pitfall 2: Missing `model-training` in rune-agent mypy_path
   366→**What goes wrong:** mypy reports `Cannot find implementation or library stub for module named "model_training"` even after adding the workspace dep.
   367→**Why it happens:** `rune-agent/pyproject.toml` has an explicit `mypy_path` list that must include `../model-training/src`.
   368→**How to avoid:** Add `"../model-training/src"` to BOTH `[tool.mypy] mypy_path` AND `[tool.pytest.ini_options] pythonpath` in `rune-agent/pyproject.toml`.
   369→**Warning signs:** `uv run mypy .` from within `services/rune-agent/` reports missing stubs for `model_training`.
   370→
   371→### Pitfall 3: Test rewrites — existing tests assert NotImplementedError
   372→**What goes wrong:** The existing tests in `test_nodes.py` and `test_trajectory.py` assert `NotImplementedError` is raised. After implementing the functions, those tests will fail (they no longer raise).
   373→**Why it happens:** Red-phase TDD tests must be rewritten to green-phase behavior tests.
   374→**How to avoid:** Replace the `pytest.raises(NotImplementedError)` assertions with behavior assertions. For `test_generate_node`, mock `get_provider()`. For `test_execute_node`, pass actual simple Python code. For `test_trajectory`, use a `tmp_path` fixture for file I/O.
   375→**Warning signs:** Tests pass when they shouldn't (test checking for the wrong error type).
   376→
   377→### Pitfall 4: Code extraction regex — no code block in response
   378→**What goes wrong:** Some LLM responses don't wrap code in ```python blocks, causing extraction to return empty string.
   379→**Why it happens:** LLMs vary in response formatting.
   380→**How to avoid:** The locked fallback is: "treat entire response as code if no blocks found." Implement with `re.search(...) or full_text`.
   381→**Warning signs:** `generated_code` is empty string, execute_node runs an empty file, tests fail silently.
   382→
   383→### Pitfall 5: save_trajectory_node state keys — session_id not in current RuneState
   384→**What goes wrong:** `state["session_id"]` raises KeyError because `session_id` is not yet in the TypedDict.
   385→**Why it happens:** AGENT-05 requires adding this field. If state.py is not updated first, save_trajectory_node cannot compile (mypy error) and will KeyError at runtime.
   386→**How to avoid:** Add `session_id: str` to `RuneState` BEFORE implementing `save_trajectory_node`. This is a dependency ordering issue — state.py update must be Wave 0 or first task.
   387→**Warning signs:** mypy error `TypedDict "RuneState" has no key "session_id"`.
   388→
   389→### Pitfall 6: record_trajectory — trajectory dict does not include task_description
   390→**What goes wrong:** The trajectory JSON written by `record_trajectory()` is missing `task_description` and `task_type` — needed for `format_for_sft()` to reconstruct the user message.
   391→**Why it happens:** The function signature is `record_trajectory(session_id, steps, outcome)` — task metadata is not passed as a parameter.
   392→**How to avoid:** Either (a) add `task_description` and `task_type` to the call from `save_trajectory_node` (signature change), or (b) accept them as optional kwargs. The CONTEXT.md says "session-level metadata: session_id, task_description, task_type, adapter_ids, outcome, timestamp, steps[]" — so these must be in the JSON. The simplest resolution: `save_trajectory_node` passes them; extend the function signature. This is a design gap that the planner must resolve.
   393→**Warning signs:** `format_for_sft()` can't find `task_description` in the trajectory dict; user turn is empty.
   394→

   501→## State of the Art
   502→
   503→| Old Approach | Current Approach | When Changed | Impact |
   504→|--------------|------------------|--------------|--------|
   505→| Custom retry loops | LangGraph StateGraph with conditional edges | Phase pre-work | `should_retry()` + `create_graph()` already complete; nodes just need implementation |
   506→| Direct VLLMClient calls | InferenceProvider.generate() (backend-agnostic) | Phase 19 | generate_node never imports VLLMProvider directly; calls get_provider() |
   507→| Red-phase test stubs (assert NotImplementedError) | Green-phase behavior tests | Phase 20 (this phase) | Tests must be rewritten, not just extended |
   508→
   509→**Deprecated/outdated:**
   510→- Direct `VLLMClient` usage: Deleted in Phase 19; replaced by `inference.factory.get_provider()`. Do not resurrect.
   511→- `adapter_loader.py`, `completion.py`, `vllm_client.py`: Deleted in Phase 19. Do not reference.

   513→## Open Questions
   514→
   515→1. **record_trajectory signature gap — task_description not in current signature**
   516→   - What we know: CONTEXT.md says JSON must contain `task_description`, `task_type`, `adapter_ids`; current stub signature is `(session_id, steps, outcome)` only.
   517→   - What's unclear: Should `save_trajectory_node` pass extra kwargs to `record_trajectory()`, or should the function pull them from the steps list (which only has code/stdout/stderr)?
   518→   - Recommendation: Extend `record_trajectory()` to accept `task_description: str = ""`, `task_type: str = ""`, `adapter_ids: list[str] | None = None` as optional parameters. `save_trajectory_node` reads them from `state` and passes them. This is a small, non-breaking signature extension.
   519→
   520→2. **Model name for generate_node**
   521→   - What we know: VLLMProvider.generate() takes a `model` parameter; the project targets `Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-7B`.
   522→   - What's unclear: Should the model name be hardcoded, env-var-configurable, or come from RuneState?
   523→   - Recommendation: Env var `RUNE_MODEL` with fallback `"Qwen/Qwen2.5-Coder-7B"`. Consistent with how VLLM_BASE_URL and INFERENCE_PROVIDER are handled. Claude's discretion on this.
   524→
   525→3. **load_trajectory() implementation scope**
   526→   - What we know: The stub exists; `test_trajectory.py` only tests that it raises NotImplementedError currently.
   527→   - What's unclear: Is load_trajectory needed for Phase 20 behavior tests, or is it tested as a stub?
   528→   - Recommendation: Implement load_trajectory alongside record_trajectory (it's trivial: read + json.loads). The test should be rewritten to verify it returns correct data. This avoids leaving a stub that will confuse Phase 21.

   178→
   179→**Helper functions (private):**
   180→
   181→`_build_prompt(state: RuneState) -> str`:
   182→- First attempt (attempt_count == 0): task_description + test_suite + instruction to write solution
   183→- Retry attempt (attempt_count > 0): same + prior generated_code + stdout + stderr + exit_code with "Your previous attempt produced the following errors:" prefix
   184→
   185→`_extract_code(text: str) -> str`:
   186→- `re.search(r"```python\s*(.*?)```", text, re.DOTALL)`
   187→- If match: return `match.group(1).strip()`
   188→- Fallback: return `text.strip()`
   189→
   190→**generate_node(state) -> dict[str, Any]:**
   191→- Get provider via `get_provider()` (not at module level -- call inside function)
   192→- Build prompt via `_build_prompt(state)`
   193→- Read model from env var `RUNE_MODEL` with fallback `DEFAULT_MODEL` (inside function body for monkeypatch)
   194→- Determine adapter_id: `state["adapter_ids"][0] if state["adapter_ids"] else None`
   195→- Call `await provider.generate(prompt=full_prompt, model=model, adapter_id=adapter_id)`
   196→  - full_prompt should be system prompt + user prompt concatenated, OR pass system_prompt separately -- since InferenceProvider.generate() takes a single `prompt` param, concatenate: `f"{SYSTEM_PROMPT}\n\n{user_prompt}"`
   197→- Extract code via `_extract_code(result.text)`
   198→- Return `{"generated_code": extracted_code}`
   199→- Log the generation at INFO level
   200→
   201→**execute_node(state) -> dict[str, Any]:**
   202→- Read timeout from env var `RUNE_EXEC_TIMEOUT` with fallback `DEFAULT_TIMEOUT` (inside function body)
   203→- Concatenate: `state["generated_code"] + "\n\n" + state["test_suite"]`
   204→- Use `tempfile.TemporaryDirectory()` context manager (auto-cleanup per Claude's discretion)
   205→- Write concatenated script to `{tmpdir}/solution.py`
   206→- Run `subprocess.run(["python", script_path], capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=timeout, cwd=tmpdir)`
   207→- Return `{"stdout": proc.stdout, "stderr": proc.stderr, "exit_code": proc.returncode, "tests_passed": proc.returncode == 0}`
   208→- Handle `subprocess.TimeoutExpired`: return `{"stdout": "", "stderr": f"Execution timed out after {timeout}s", "exit_code": 1, "tests_passed": False}`
   209→- Log execution result at INFO level
   210→
   211→**reflect_node(state) -> dict[str, Any]:**
   212→- Build step dict: `{"generated_code": ..., "stdout": ..., "stderr": ..., "exit_code": ..., "tests_passed": ...}` from state
   213→- Return `{"attempt_count": state["attempt_count"] + 1, "trajectory": state["trajectory"] + [step]}`
   214→  - CRITICAL: Use `state["trajectory"] + [step]` (new list), NOT `.append()` -- LangGraph requires immutable state updates
   215→- Log at INFO level with attempt number
   216→
   217→**save_trajectory_node(state) -> dict[str, Any]:**
   218→- Determine outcome: `"success" if state["tests_passed"] else "exhausted"`
   219→- Call `record_trajectory(session_id=state["session_id"], steps=state["trajectory"], outcome=outcome, task_description=state["task_description"], task_type=state["task_type"], adapter_ids=state["adapter_ids"])`
   220→- Return `{"outcome": outcome}`
   221→- Log at INFO level with session_id and outcome

Found 2 plans in `.planning/phases/20-agent-loop` (2 incomplete)

## Execution Plan

**Phase 20: Agent Loop** — 2 plans across 2 waves

| Wave | Plans | What it builds |
|------|-------|----------------|
| 1 | 20-01 | Trajectory persistence lib + RuneState prep |
| 2 | 20-02 | All 4 node implementations + green tests |

---
## Wave 1

**20-01: Trajectory Persistence & Agent Config**
Implements `record_trajectory()`, `load_trajectory()`, and `format_for_sft()` in the model-training library — the trajectory persistence layer that saves structured JSON to `~/.rune/trajectories/`. Also adds `session_id` to `RuneState` and wires `model-training` as a workspace dependency of `rune-agent`. This is the foundation Plan 20-02 depends on for `save_trajectory_node`.

Spawning 1 agent...

    58→Recent decisions affecting v5.0:
    59→
    60→- vllm/vllm-openai:v0.16.0 base image used; openai removed from pip install (bundled in vLLM image); VLLM_ALLOW_RUNTIME_LORA_UPDATING=True enables runtime adapter loading
    61→- lora-server host port changed from 8000 to 8100 in docker-compose; container-internal port stays 8000 (startup.sh unchanged)
    62→- rune_data:/data shared volume added to api and lora-server in docker-compose for cross-service SQLite access
    63→- Hardware validation deferred — user will validate hardware separately; not a roadmap phase
    64→- Provider abstraction added — InferenceProvider interface (INF-01) with VLLMProvider (INF-02), OllamaProvider (INF-03), factory (INF-04), per-step model/provider config (INF-07)
    65→- AGENT-01 is backend-agnostic — calls InferenceProvider.generate(), not VLLMClient directly
    66→- Build order: adapter-registry → inference abstraction + providers → agent loop + trajectory → QLoRA training → kill-switch gate
    67→- INFRA-01/02/03 in Phase 19 (lora-server config + port fix); INFRA-04/05 in Phase 21 (GPU deps)
    68→- evolution-svc explicitly out of scope for v5.0 (deferred to v6+)
    69→- bfloat16 hardcoded in build_qlora_config (float16 causes silent NaN loss at 7B scale)
    70→- max_loras=2, max_lora_rank=64, gpu_memory_utilization=0.80 to avoid VRAM OOM at startup
    71→- Engine from sqlalchemy.engine not sqlmodel — SQLModel does not re-export Engine (discovered 18-01, confirmed 18-02)
    72→- Session-per-method pattern with expire_on_commit=False + expunge() — required to prevent DetachedInstanceError when returning records from closed sessions (established 18-01)
    73→- WAL test must use file-based engine (tmp_path), not :memory: — WAL has no practical effect on in-memory SQLite

"""Green-phase behavior tests for model_training.trajectory module."""

from __future__ import annotations

import json
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any

import pytest
from model_training.trajectory import format_for_sft, load_trajectory, record_trajectory


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

SAMPLE_STEPS: list[dict[str, Any]] = [
    {
        "attempt": 0,
        "generated_code": "def add(a, b):\n    return a + b",
        "stdout": "",
        "stderr": "AssertionError",
        "exit_code": 1,
        "tests_passed": False,
    },
    {
        "attempt": 1,
        "generated_code": "def add(a, b):\n    return a + b\n\nassert add(1, 2) == 3",
        "stdout": "",
        "stderr": "",
        "exit_code": 0,
        "tests_passed": True,
    },
]


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# record_trajectory tests
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


def test_record_trajectory_writes_json(tmp_path: Path, monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch) -> None:
    """record_trajectory writes a JSON file to the configured trajectory directory."""
    monkeypatch.setenv("RUNE_TRAJECTORY_DIR", str(tmp_path))
    record_trajectory("sess-001", SAMPLE_STEPS, outcome="success")
    expected_file = tmp_path / "sess-001.json"
    assert expected_file.exists(), "Expected JSON file was not created"
    data = json.loads(expected_file.read_text())
    assert data["session_id"] == "sess-001"
    assert data["outcome"] == "success"
    assert "timestamp" in data
    assert len(data["steps"]) == 2


def test_record_trajectory_returns_metadata(tmp_path: Path, monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch) -> None:
    """record_trajectory returns dict with session_id and file_path keys."""
    monkeypatch.setenv("RUNE_TRAJECTORY_DIR", str(tmp_path))
    result = record_trajectory("sess-002", [], outcome="exhausted")
    assert result["session_id"] == "sess-002"
    assert "file_path" in result
    assert Path(result["file_path"]).exists()


def test_record_trajectory_creates_directory(tmp_path: Path, monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch) -> None:
    """record_trajectory creates the directory if it does not exist."""
    subdir = tmp_path / "nested" / "trajectories"
    monkeypatch.setenv("RUNE_TRAJECTORY_DIR", str(subdir))
    assert not subdir.exists(), "Directory should not exist yet"
    record_trajectory("sess-003", [], outcome="success")
    assert subdir.exists(), "Directory should have been created"
    assert (subdir / "sess-003.json").exists()


def test_record_trajectory_includes_task_metadata(tmp_path: Path, monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch) -> None:
    """record_trajectory includes task_description, task_type, and adapter_ids in JSON."""
    monkeypatch.setenv("RUNE_TRAJECTORY_DIR", str(tmp_path))
    record_trajectory(
        "sess-004",
        SAMPLE_STEPS,
        outcome="success",
        task_description="Write an add function",
        task_type="function",
        adapter_ids=["adapter-001", "adapter-002"],
    )
    data = json.loads((tmp_path / "sess-004.json").read_text())
    assert data["task_description"] == "Write an add function"
    assert data["task_type"] == "function"
    assert data["adapter_ids"] == ["adapter-001", "adapter-002"]


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# load_trajectory tests
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


def test_load_trajectory_reads_recorded(tmp_path: Path, monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch) -> None:
    """load_trajectory reads back a previously recorded trajectory (round-trip)."""
    monkeypatch.setenv("RUNE_TRAJECTORY_DIR", str(tmp_path))
    record_trajectory(
        "sess-005",
        SAMPLE_STEPS,
        outcome="success",
        task_description="Round-trip test",
        task_type="function",
        adapter_ids=["adapter-abc"],
    )
    loaded = load_trajectory("sess-005")
    assert loaded["session_id"] == "sess-005"
    assert loaded["outcome"] == "success"
    assert loaded["task_description"] == "Round-trip test"
    assert len(loaded["steps"]) == 2


def test_load_trajectory_missing_raises(tmp_path: Path, monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch) -> None:
    """load_trajectory raises FileNotFoundError for non-existent session_id."""
    monkeypatch.setenv("RUNE_TRAJECTORY_DIR", str(tmp_path))
    with pytest.raises(FileNotFoundError):
        load_trajectory("does-not-exist")


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# format_for_sft tests
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------


def _make_trajectory(outcome: str, steps: list[dict[str, Any]], task_description: str = "Write an add function") -> dict[str, Any]:
    """Helper to create a trajectory dict for format_for_sft tests."""
    return {
        "session_id": "sess-test",
        "outcome": outcome,
        "task_description": task_description,
        "steps": steps,
    }


def test_format_for_sft_success() -> None:
    """format_for_sft returns [system, user, assistant] messages for a successful trajectory."""
    trajectory = _make_trajectory("success", SAMPLE_STEPS, task_description="Write an add function")
    messages = format_for_sft(trajectory)
    assert len(messages) == 3
    assert messages[0]["role"] == "system"
    assert messages[1]["role"] == "user"
    assert messages[1]["content"] == "Write an add function"
    assert messages[2]["role"] == "assistant"
    # assistant content should be from the last step with tests_passed=True
    assert "tests_passed" not in messages[2]["content"]  # it's code, not a dict
    assert "def add" in messages[2]["content"]


def test_format_for_sft_exhausted_returns_empty() -> None:
    """format_for_sft returns empty list for trajectories with outcome != 'success'."""
    trajectory = _make_trajectory("exhausted", SAMPLE_STEPS)
    result = format_for_sft(trajectory)
    assert result == []


def test_format_for_sft_no_successful_step_returns_empty() -> None:
    """format_for_sft returns empty list if outcome=success but no step has tests_passed=True."""
    failing_steps: list[dict[str, Any]] = [
        {"attempt": 0, "generated_code": "broken", "tests_passed": False},
        {"attempt": 1, "generated_code": "still broken", "tests_passed": False},
    ]
    trajectory = _make_trajectory("success", failing_steps)
    result = format_for_sft(trajectory)
    assert result == []

"""Trajectory recording and formatting for coding session distillation.

Provides functions to persist, load, and convert coding session trajectories
into SFT-compatible chat format for LoRA fine-tuning pipelines.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import json
import os
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any, Optional

SYSTEM_PROMPT = "You are a Python code generator. Output only code, no explanation."


def _get_trajectory_dir() -> Path:
    """Return the trajectory storage directory, respecting RUNE_TRAJECTORY_DIR env var.

    Reads env var inside function body (not module level) so that monkeypatch.setenv()
    works correctly in tests.
    """
    env_dir = os.environ.get("RUNE_TRAJECTORY_DIR")
    if env_dir:
        return Path(env_dir)
    return Path.home() / ".rune" / "trajectories"


def record_trajectory(
    session_id: str,
    steps: list[dict[str, Any]],
    outcome: Optional[str] = None,
    *,
    task_description: str = "",
    task_type: str = "",
    adapter_ids: list[str] | None = None,
) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """Persist a coding session trajectory to disk for future distillation.

    Args:
        session_id: Unique identifier for the coding session.
        steps: List of step dicts, each containing attempt results.
        outcome: Final session result ('success', 'exhausted', or None).
        task_description: Natural language description of the coding task.
        task_type: Category of task (e.g. 'function', 'class', 'refactor').
        adapter_ids: LoRA adapter IDs used during the session.

    Returns:
        A dict with 'session_id' and 'file_path' keys.
    """
    trajectory_dir = _get_trajectory_dir()
    trajectory_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    file_path = trajectory_dir / f"{session_id}.json"
    timestamp = datetime.now(tz=timezone.utc).isoformat()

    trajectory: dict[str, Any] = {
        "session_id": session_id,
        "task_description": task_description,
        "task_type": task_type,
        "adapter_ids": adapter_ids if adapter_ids is not None else [],
        "outcome": outcome,
        "timestamp": timestamp,
        "steps": steps,
    }

    file_path.write_text(json.dumps(trajectory, indent=2))

    return {"session_id": session_id, "file_path": str(file_path)}


def load_trajectory(trajectory_id: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """Load a stored trajectory by session ID.

    Args:
        trajectory_id: The session ID used as the filename (without .json).

    Returns:
        A dict containing the full trajectory data including steps and metadata.

    Raises:
        FileNotFoundError: If no trajectory file exists for the given ID.
    """
    trajectory_dir = _get_trajectory_dir()
    file_path = trajectory_dir / f"{trajectory_id}.json"
    # Let FileNotFoundError propagate naturally if file does not exist
    return json.loads(file_path.read_text())  # type: ignore[no-any-return]


def format_for_sft(trajectory: dict[str, Any]) -> list[dict[str, str]]:
    """Convert a trajectory into SFT-compatible chat format.

    Only successful trajectories (outcome == 'success') produce output.
    Extracts the final step where tests_passed is True as the assistant message.

    Args:
        trajectory: A trajectory dict as returned by load_trajectory.

    Returns:
        A list of 3 message dicts ([system, user, assistant]) for successful
        trajectories, or an empty list if the trajectory did not succeed.
    """
    if trajectory.get("outcome") != "success":
        return []

    steps: list[dict[str, Any]] = trajectory.get("steps", [])
    successful_step = next(
        (s for s in reversed(steps) if s.get("tests_passed")),
        None,
    )

    if successful_step is None:
        return []

    task_description: str = trajectory.get("task_description", "")
    generated_code: str = successful_step.get("generated_code", "")

    return [
        {"role": "system", "content": SYSTEM_PROMPT},
        {"role": "user", "content": task_description},
        {"role": "assistant", "content": generated_code},
    ]

        stdout: Standard output from executing generated code.
        stderr: Standard error from executing generated code.
        exit_code: Process exit code from execution (0 = success).
        tests_passed: Whether the test suite passed on this attempt.
        trajectory: List of per-attempt records for parametric memory training.
        outcome: Terminal result -- 'success', 'exhausted', or None if still running.
    """

    # Task intake
    task_description: str
    task_type: str
    test_suite: str
    adapter_ids: list[str]
    # Loop tracking
    attempt_count: int
    max_attempts: int
input.new_string:
class RuneState(TypedDict):
    """State for the Rune coding agent recursive loop.

    Attributes:
        task_description: Natural language description of the coding task.
        task_type: Category of task (e.g. 'function', 'class', 'refactor').
        test_suite: Test code that the generated solution must pass.
        adapter_ids: LoRA adapter IDs to load for parametric memory.
        session_id: Unique identifier for trajectory persistence (UUID4).
        attempt_count: Current attempt number (0-indexed, incremented by reflect).
        max_attempts: Maximum number of generation attempts allowed.
        generated_code: Code produced by the generate node.
        stdout: Standard output from executing generated code.
        stderr: Standard error from executing generated code.
        exit_code: Process exit code from execution (0 = success).
        tests_passed: Whether the test suite passed on this attempt.
        trajectory: List of per-attempt records for parametric memory training.
        outcome: Terminal result -- 'success', 'exhausted', or None if still running.
    """

    # Task intake
    task_description: str
    task_type: str
    test_suite: str
    adapter_ids: list[str]
    # Loop tracking
    session_id: str
    attempt_count: int
    max_attempts: int

   132→<frontmatter_guidance>
   133→**Purpose:** Enable automatic context assembly via dependency graph. Frontmatter makes summary metadata machine-readable so plan-phase can scan all summaries quickly and select relevant ones based on dependencies.
   134→
   135→**Fast scanning:** Frontmatter is first ~25 lines, cheap to scan across all summaries without reading full content.

   143→**Key-files:** Important files for @context references in PLAN.md.

   145→**Patterns:** Established conventions future phases should maintain.
   146→

   151→The one-liner MUST be substantive:
   152→

   155→- "Prisma schema with User, Session, and Product models"

   193→
   194→## Decisions Made
   195→- Used jose instead of jsonwebtoken (ESM-native, Edge-compatible)
   196→- 15-min access tokens with 7-day refresh tokens

# Phase 20 Plan 01: Trajectory Persistence Library Summary

**Trajectory JSON persistence (record/load/format_for_sft) with SFT chat formatting, session_id in RuneState, and model-training wired as rune-agent workspace dependency**

## Performance

- **Duration:** 3 min
- **Started:** 2026-03-05T15:52:35Z
- **Completed:** 2026-03-05T15:55:09Z
- **Tasks:** 2
- **Files modified:** 5

## Accomplishments
- Implemented record_trajectory(), load_trajectory(), format_for_sft() replacing all NotImplementedError stubs
- 9 green-phase behavior tests covering file I/O, round-trip, error cases, and SFT formatting — all passing
- Added session_id: str field to RuneState TypedDict for trajectory linkage
- Wired model-training as workspace dependency in rune-agent (pyproject.toml all 4 locations)

## Task Commits

Each task was committed atomically:

1. **Task 1: Implement trajectory library (record, load, format_for_sft)** - `099e59a` (feat)
2. **Task 2: Add session_id to RuneState and wire model-training dependency** - `abfaadd` (feat)

**Plan metadata:** (final docs commit, see below)

_Note: Task 1 used TDD pattern (RED then GREEN)_

## Files Created/Modified
- `libs/model-training/src/model_training/trajectory.py` - Full implementation: record_trajectory writes JSON, load_trajectory reads by ID, format_for_sft converts to SFT chat messages
- `libs/model-training/src/model_training/__init__.py` - Re-exports all 3 trajectory functions with __all__
- `libs/model-training/tests/test_trajectory.py` - 9 behavior tests (replaced 3 NotImplementedError tests)
- `services/rune-agent/src/rune_agent/state.py` - Added session_id: str field and docstring
- `services/rune-agent/pyproject.toml` - Added model-training to dependencies, mypy_path, pythonpath, uv.sources

## Decisions Made
- Env var RUNE_TRAJECTORY_DIR read inside function body (not module level) so monkeypatch.setenv() works in tests — same pattern established in Phase 19 factory.py
- record_trajectory() signature extended with keyword-only args (task_description, task_type, adapter_ids) — save_trajectory_node (Plan 02) will pass these directly from RuneState
- format_for_sft() uses reversed(steps) to find last step with tests_passed=True as assistant content, handling multi-attempt sessions correctly
- SYSTEM_PROMPT defined as module constant "You are a Python code generator. Output only code, no explanation." — same prompt used in generate_node

## Deviations from Plan

None - plan executed exactly as written.

## Issues Encountered

None.

## User Setup Required

None - no external service configuration required.

## Next Phase Readiness
- Trajectory persistence infrastructure complete — save_trajectory_node (Plan 02) can call record_trajectory() and format_for_sft() directly
- RuneState.session_id field ready for UUID4 assignment in graph initialization
- model-training importable from rune-agent service code

content.text:
## PLAN COMPLETE

**Plan:** 20-01
**Tasks:** 2/2
**SUMMARY:** `/Users/noahdolevelixir/Code/rune/.planning/phases/20-agent-loop/20-01-SUMMARY.md`

**Commits:**
- `099e59a`: feat(20-01): implement trajectory library (record, load, format_for_sft)
- `abfaadd`: feat(20-01): add session_id to RuneState and wire model-training dependency
- `edb3b77`: docs(20-01): complete trajectory-persistence-and-session-id plan

**Duration:** ~3 min

**What shipped:**

Task 1 (TDD): Replaced all `NotImplementedError` stubs in `libs/model-training/src/model_training/trajectory.py` with working implementations:
- `record_trajectory()` — writes `~/.rune/trajectories/{session_id}.json` (or `RUNE_TRAJECTORY_DIR`), creates directory if missing, accepts keyword-only `task_description`/`task_type`/`adapter_ids`
- `load_trajectory()` — reads JSON by session_id, propagates `FileNotFoundError` naturally
- `format_for_sft()` — returns `[system, user, assistant]` 3-message list for `outcome=="success"` with `tests_passed=True` step, empty list otherwise
- `__init__.py` updated with re-exports and `__all__`
- 9 green-phase behavior tests all passing

Task 2: Added `session_id: str` to `RuneState` TypedDict and wired `model-training` as workspace dependency in `services/rune-agent/pyproject.toml` (all 4 locations: dependencies, mypy_path, pythonpath, uv.sources). All existing graph tests remain passing. mypy and ruff clean.

content.text:
Spot-checks passed: SUMMARY exists, 3 commits present, no self-check failures.

---
## Wave 1 Complete

**20-01: Trajectory Persistence & Agent Config**
Trajectory library implemented with `record_trajectory()`, `load_trajectory()`, `format_for_sft()` — 9 green tests passing. `session_id` added to `RuneState`, `model-training` wired as workspace dependency. Wave 2 can now import `record_trajectory` from `save_trajectory_node`.

---
## Wave 2

**20-02: Node Implementations & Green Tests**
Implements all 4 agent loop node functions: `generate_node` (calls `InferenceProvider.generate()` with self-repair prompting), `execute_node` (subprocess sandbox in tmpdir with 30s timeout), `reflect_node` (accumulates trajectory data, no LLM call), and `save_trajectory_node` (calls `record_trajectory()` from the trajectory lib just shipped in Wave 1). Rewrites all red-phase `test_nodes.py` assertions to verify correct behavior.

Spawning 1 agent...

   157→import logging
